source_provenance: Public web source-gate discovery only; no market-bar backtest was run because the timestamp-safe event-source prerequisite failed. No credentials, daemon, orders, CSV market data, raw bars, caches, or sensitive local paths are stored.
candidate_pool_preperformance: Russell 1000 or equivalent large-cap Russell public additions/deletions

source_gate.reason: Could not obtain a reproducible corpus of at least 150 timestamp-safe tradable liquid large-cap common-stock index addition/deletion announcement events with announcement date/time or a conservative next-session lag. Available public sources either expose effective dates without announcement timestamps, only current-year lists, sparse annual Nasdaq-100 press releases, or blocked/paywalled/index-notice pages that cannot be used as a durable reproducible event file. Per AR-141, no OHLCV/effective-date inference was allowed.

source_coverage_findings.source: Wikipedia List of S&P 500 companies, selected changes table
source_coverage_findings.finding: The selected-changes table is broad and machine-readable but its event date is an effective date. It does not provide announcement date/time or a durable as-of timestamp for each addition/deletion, so using it would violate the source gate.

source_coverage_findings.source: Nasdaq public annual Nasdaq-100 reconstitution press releases
source_coverage_findings.finding: Recent annual press releases include public announcement dates, effective dates before market open, and named adds/removes, but the durable accessible sample found during the gate was sparse and far below 150 by itself. It also covers only one index family and would not satisfy breadth/no-dominance requirements.

source_coverage_findings.observed_records: current-year broad Russell 3000/Microcap final PDF links and calendar text
source_coverage_findings.finding: The public page gives current-year reconstitution calendar and final broad-index PDFs, not a durable historical large-cap event corpus with per-security announcement timestamps. Current-year broad Russell 3000 files are not a fixed liquid large-cap common-stock universe and cannot support random-window/leave-year evaluation.

source_coverage_findings.source: S&P Dow Jones Indices index-news/press pages
source_coverage_findings.finding: Direct public index-news discovery was not reproducibly accessible from this environment; the accessible S&P coverage found was effective-date oriented, not an event-timestamp corpus.

decision.rationale: Hard source gate required >=150 timestamp-safe tradable large-cap common-stock index addition/deletion announcement events before any return scoring. The gate failed, so null metrics are reported honestly and no child issue is proposed.
